nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; inhabits holes and crevices in coral and rock reefs across tide pools, lagoonal reefs, and reef slopes at depths reported around 5–40 m. Generally observed as cryptic, solitary individuals or in pairs.

diet.notes: Generally regarded as a meaty-food carnivore. Has been reported to readily accept vitamin-enriched mysis and brine shrimp, finely chopped marine seafood, and quality marine flake or pellet preparations. Once-daily feeding is typically advised, with multiple smaller feedings sometimes recommended for newly introduced specimens.

avoidWith: fish of similar shape or color

summary: A small Western Pacific dottyback (Pseudochromidae) from the Coral Sea, generally considered hardy and beginner-friendly. Reef-tolerant but semi-aggressive; tends to attack ornamental shrimp and other dottybacks.
careNotes:
The Mccullochi Dottyback is generally considered one of the more straightforward saltwater fish to keep, taking meaty foods readily and adapting quickly to captivity once it locates a defended cave or crevice in the rockwork. It tends to be cryptic at first, then patrols mid-water near its territory once settled.

A secure lid is generally advised — dottybacks as a group are known jumpers, and most hobby sources recommend a well-fitted top for any pseudochromid setup. Provide ample live rock with caves, overhangs, and crevices for shelter and supplemental grazing.

Temperament is consistently described as semi-aggressive: only one specimen (or a bonded pair) is generally advised per aquarium, and conspecifics or fish of similar shape and color should be avoided. The species has been reported to attack ornamental shrimp and may bully smaller, more docile tankmates; otherwise it is generally regarded as reef-safe with corals and most sessile invertebrates.

Cross-source notes: adult size — FishBase 7.5 cm TL, LiveAquaria 4 in (10 cm), BlueZoo 3 in (7.6 cm), Reeflings 15 cm (outlier, judged unreliable against FishBase taxonomic authority). Adopted ~6–10 cm. Tank size — LiveAquaria/BlueZoo 30 US gal (114 L), Bulk Reef Supply general 20–30 gal, Reeflings 200 L, LiveAquaria pair recommendation 100 gal (380 L). Adopted 150 L as a cautious round value above the retail baseline, reflecting territoriality and pair-keeping advice. Temperature — LiveAquaria 72–78°F (22–26°C) vs Reeflings 24–27°C; averaged to 23–26°C. Native range — FishBase (Great Barrier Reef, PNG, Tonga) preferred over Reeflings' (Red Sea–Line Islands) which conflicts with the type locality.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding is generally considered challenging in home aquariums, in part due to strong intraspecific aggression typical of the family. Like most pseudochromids, the species is believed to be a protogynous (female-first) sequential hermaphrodite — the largest female has been reported to undergo a sex change if the dominant male is lost. Pairs are typically formed by introducing a smaller individual to a larger established one.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Used for care level (Easy), temperament (semi-aggressive), diet (carnivore — meaty foods), reef-compatible (yes), water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12), 30 US gal minimum tank baseline, 4-inch adult size, Coral Sea native range, and pair-tank guidance (≥100 gal).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for accepted scientific name Cypho purpurascens (De Vis, 1884), family Pseudochromidae, order Ovalentaria, max length 7.5 cm TL, depth range 5–40 m, native distribution (Great Barrier Reef, Papua New Guinea, Tonga), reef-associated marine tropical habitat, and IUCN Least Concern (2015).

additional.name: Reeflings
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (24–27°C), pH (8.1–8.4), SG (1.020–1.025), and confirmed protogynous (female-first) sequential hermaphroditism with harem formation. Reported adult size of 15 cm and 'peaceful' temperament were treated as outliers against FishBase/LiveAquaria/BlueZoo consensus and were not adopted; native-range claim (Red Sea to Line Islands) was also rejected in favor of FishBase.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Cross-checked 30 US gal minimum, beginner care level, semi-aggressive temperament, carnivorous diet, reef-safe yes, and Coral Sea native range. Reported max size of 3 inches sat at the lower end of the size spread.

additional.name: Bulk Reef Supply
additional.notes: Genus/family-level care guide used for jumping/sealed-lid recommendation, conspecific-aggression advice (one dottyback per aquarium), and 20–30 US gal general minimum range applicable to small pseudochromids.

fish.breedingNotes: Generally considered difficult to breed in home aquaria due to intraspecific aggression characteristic of pseudochromids. Reported to be a protogynous (female-first) sequential hermaphrodite that forms harems in the wild; pairs are usually formed by introducing a smaller individual to a larger established one.
